title: Eriobotrya buisanensis
intro: Eriobotrya buisanensis, also known as Buisan pear or Japanese plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Eriobotrya buisanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eriobotrya buisanensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in formulations for the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions.
